Fixed! The issue was that `parts[p]` and `parts[parts.length - 1]` could be `undefined`, causing type errors on lines 10 and 15. I fixed this by:

1. Adding a null check for each part before processing it (`if (!part) continue;`)
2. Using the nullish coalescing operator to provide a default empty string for the last part (`?? ""`)

All tests pass ✅ and TypeScript compilation is clean.
